revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[hr:Tōdai-ji Daibucu]][[hu:A narai Nagy Buddha]][[ja:東大寺盧舎那仏像]][[ru:Большой Будда в Наре]][[uk:Великий Будда у Нарі]]
{{Redirect|Nara Daibutsu|the statue in Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture|Nara Daibutsu (Ichihara City)}} [[File:Daibutsu of Todaiji 4.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Great Buddha of Tōdai-ji (Roshana Buddha Statue)]] The '''Great Buddha of Tōdai-ji''' (東大寺盧舎那仏像, ''Tōdai-ji Roshana Butsuzō'') is the principal image of [[Tōdai-ji]] Temple’s Great Buddha Hall (Kon-dō) in [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a [[:en:Buddha statue|Buddha statue]] (Daibutsu) commonly known as the '''Tōdai-ji Daibutsu'''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Everything about the Great Buddha {{!}} Nara City Tourism Association Website|url=https://narashikanko.or.jp/feature/daibutsu/|accessdate=2021-06-29|language=ja}}</ref> or the '''Nara Daibutsu'''.
Commissioned by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} in the 17th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} era (745 CE), its construction began and culminated in a consecration ceremony (Kaigen Kuyō-e) in the 4th year of the [[:en:Tenpyō Shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] era (752 CE). Although the statue suffered multiple fires over the centuries, leaving most parts reconstructed, some original portions remain. It has been designated a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] under the title "Bronze Seated Statue of Roshana Buddha."
== Overview == [[File:Todaiji02.JPG|thumb|left|The Great Buddha of Tōdai-ji]] [[File:Daibutsu Engi Emaki (Todaiji).jpg|thumb|250px|From ''Daibutsu Engi'' (Illustrated Origins of the Great Buddha)]] [[File:Hōkōji Daibutsu Kaempfer.png|thumb|220px|Sketch of the Hōkō-ji Great Buddha (Kyoto Daibutsu) by Engelbert Kaempfer, held at the British Museum.<ref>Beatrice M. Bodart-Bailey, ''Kaempfer and Tokugawa Tsunayoshi: German Physician and Shogun,'' Chūō Kōronsha, 1994, p. 95.</ref><ref>Kaempfer, translated by Noboru Saitō, ''The History of Japan,'' Heibonsha, 1977, p. 228-231.</ref>]] [[File:お身拭い.jpg|thumb|The annual "Omi-fuki" cleaning of the Great Buddha in August]]
The Great Buddha of Tōdai-ji was envisioned by Emperor Shōmu in the 15th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} era (743 CE). Construction preparations began in 745 CE, and the consecration ceremony was held in 752 CE. Approximately 2.6 million people were involved in the construction. According to Professor Katsuhiro Miyamoto of {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|qid=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}}, the costs of building the original Buddha and hall are estimated to be equivalent to approximately 465.7 billion yen in today’s currency.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20100812082559/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/trend/100804/trd1008041905005-n1.htm The Great Buddha of Tōdai-ji, Costing 465.7 Billion Yen Today] Sankei Shimbun, August 4, 2010.</ref>
Originally planned for construction near the {{ill|Shigaraki Palace|en|Shigaraki Palace|qid=Q262438|lt=Shigaraki Palace|1=Shigaraki Palace}} in present-day [[Kōka City]], {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, the site was moved to its current location in [[Nara]] after a series of suspicious forest fires and other ominous events. Notable artisans, including the Daibutsu master {{ill|Kuninaka-no-Muraji Kimimaro|ja|国中連公麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninaka-no-Muraji Kimimaro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国中公麻呂|1=Kuninaka no Muraji Kimimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200462}} and casters such as {{ill|Takechi-no-Ōkuni|ja|高市大国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi-no-Ōkuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Takechi-no-Mamaro, contributed to its creation.
== Cultural Significance == The Great Buddha represents the cosmic Buddha, {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Vairocana|1=Vairocana}} (Roshana), as described in the ''{{ill|Buddhāvataṃsaka Sūtra|en|Buddhāvataṃsaka Sūtra|qid=Q790258|lt=Avatamsaka Sutra|1=Buddhāvataṃsaka Sūtra}}.'' This scripture, introduced to Japan during the Nara period, portrays a vision of the universe centered on Vairocana. The statue embodies Emperor Shōmu’s vision to bring stability and unity to Japan through the power of Buddhism.
== Restoration and Current State == The Great Buddha has endured significant damage and reconstructions, including two major fires during the late Heian and Sengoku periods. Current elements of the statue date back to different periods: the body from the Kamakura era and the head from the Edo period. However, parts of the lotus pedestal, right flank, and arms preserve original Nara period components. The intricately engraved lotus petals depicting scenes from the ''Avatamsaka Sutra'' are invaluable artifacts of Nara period Buddhist art.
The Tōdai-ji Great Buddha Hall, rebuilt in the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|qid=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} era (1709 CE), is recognized as one of the largest wooden buildings in the world. Though smaller in width than the original hall, it continues to symbolize Japanese architectural and cultural heritage.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Todaiji Daibutsu}} * {{ill|History of Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺の歴史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Tōdai-ji|1=history of Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526463}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji Great Buddha Hall|ja|東大寺大仏殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Great Buddha Hall|1=Great Buddha Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3012032}} * {{ill|Chōkō Copper Mine|ja|長登銅山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkō Copper Mine|1=Naganobori copper mine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11653520}} * {{ill|Kōan Copper Mine|ja|安宅銅山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōan Copper Mine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504479|1=Kōan Copper Mine}} * {{ill|An Expedition to the Great Buddha|ja|大仏開眼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An Expedition to the Great Buddha|1=Great Buddha's Eye-Opening Ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108778899}} * [[Todaiji Great Buddha Consecration (TV Drama)]]
{{Coord|34.688987|135.839837|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|東大寺盧舎那仏像|version=104340503|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:東大寺盧舎那仏像]]
